잠언 14:1.content: Every wise woman builds her house, but the foolish one tears it down with her own hands.

잠언 14:2.content: He who walks in his uprightness fears Yahweh, but he who is perverse in his ways despises him.

잠언 14:3.content: The fool’s talk brings a rod to his back, but the lips of the wise protect them.

잠언 14:4.content: Where no oxen are, the crib is clean, but much increase is by the strength of the ox.

잠언 14:5.content: A truthful witness will not lie, but a false witness pours out lies.

잠언 14:6.content: A scoffer seeks wisdom, and doesn’t find it, but knowledge comes easily to a discerning person.

잠언 14:7.content: Stay away from a foolish man, for you won’t find knowledge on his lips.

잠언 14:8.content: The wisdom of the prudent is to think about his way, but the folly of fools is deceit.

잠언 14:9.content: Fools mock at making atonement for sins, but among the upright there is good will.

잠언 14:10.content: The heart knows its own bitterness and joy; he will not share these with a stranger.

잠언 14:11.content: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, but the tent of the upright will flourish.

잠언 14:12.content: There is a way which seems right to a man, but in the end it leads to death.

잠언 14:13.content: Even in laughter the heart may be sorrowful, and mirth may end in heaviness.

잠언 14:14.content: The unfaithful will be repaid for his own ways; likewise a good man will be rewarded for his ways.

잠언 14:15.content: A simple man believes everything, but the prudent man carefully considers his ways.

잠언 14:16.content: A wise man fears and shuns evil, but the fool is hot headed and reckless.

잠언 14:17.content: He who is quick to become angry will commit folly, and a crafty man is hated.

잠언 14:18.content: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

잠언 14:19.content: The evil bow down before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.

잠언 14:20.content: The poor person is shunned even by his own neighbor, but the rich person has many friends.

잠언 14:21.content: He who despises his neighbor sins, but he who has pity on the poor is blessed.

잠언 14:22.content: Don’t they go astray who plot evil? But love and faithfulness belong to those who plan good.

잠언 14:23.content: In all hard work there is profit, but the talk of the lips leads only to poverty.

잠언 14:24.content: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the folly of fools crowns them with folly.

잠언 14:25.content: A truthful witness saves souls, but a false witness is deceitful.

잠언 14:26.content: In the fear of Yahweh is a secure fortress, and he will be a refuge for his children.

잠언 14:27.content: The fear of Yahweh is a fountain of life, turning people from the snares of death.

잠언 14:28.content: In the multitude of people is the king’s glory, but in the lack of people is the destruction of the prince.

잠언 14:29.content: He who is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a quick temper displays folly.

잠언 14:30.content: The life of the body is a heart at peace, but envy rots the bones.

잠언 14:31.content: He who oppresses the poor shows contempt for his Maker, but he who is kind to the needy honors him.

잠언 14:32.content: The wicked is brought down in his calamity, but in death, the righteous has a refuge.

잠언 14:33.content: Wisdom rests in the heart of one who has understanding, and is even made known in the inward part of fools.

잠언 14:34.content: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.

잠언 14:35.content: The king’s favor is toward a servant who deals wisely, but his wrath is toward one who causes shame.
